tasks.subject: V1: port bubble (CPU + Metal) â€” flips 05739/31943 to .gate
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Port flam3 `bubble` (var28, variations.c:671-678; paramless, 0 RNG draws) to CPU `Variations.swift` table + Metal `Kernels.metal`. Flip 05739 + 31943 from `.knownGap` to `.gate` (they use only bubble). This alone makes the motion-blur clip genomes faithful.

**Formula:** `r = weight / (0.25 * (p.x*p.x+p.y*p.y) + 1); return SIMD2(r*p.x, r*p.y)`.

**Files:** VariationDescriptor.swift (canonicalOrder + `d("bubble",[],[:])`), Variations.swift (table), Kernels.metal (NUM_XFORM_SLOTS_MS 33â†’34, `v_bubble` + dispatch line), VariationsTests.swift (closed-form hand-traced), VariationDescriptorTests.swift + ParamChannelParityTests.swift (bump 33â†’34), SpecialSauceParityTests.swift (testBubble â‰Ą38dB), RealGenomeParityTests.swift (flip 05739/31943 â†’ .gate; update 00000 reason to remove bubble).

**Acceptance Criteria:** CPU closed-form test passes (hand-traced from var28_bubble); Metalâ†”CPU parity testBubble â‰Ą38 dB; 05739+31943 flipped to .gate both â‰Ą38 dB/0.95 SSIM; make test-fast green; goldens unchanged (additive).

**Verify:** swift test --filter VariationsTests --filter VariationDescriptorTests --filter ParamChannelParityTests --filter SpecialSauceParityTests --filter RealGenomeParityTests (sandbox OFF).

tasks.subject: V2: port eyefish (CPU + Metal; NOT a fisheye alias)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Port flam3 `eyefish` (var27, variations.c:659-669; paramless, 0 RNG draws). NOT an alias of fisheye â€” output is UN-SWAPPED `(r*p.x, r*p.y)` vs fisheye's swapped `(r*p.y, r*p.x)`. Unblocks 00000 (partially).

**Formula:** `r = (weight*2)/(sqrt(p.x*p.x+p.y*p.y) + 1); return SIMD2(r*p.x, r*p.y)` (no axis swap).

**Files:** VariationDescriptor.swift (canonicalOrder + descriptor; countâ†’35), Variations.swift (table), Kernels.metal (countâ†’35, v_eyefish + dispatch), VariationsTests.swift (closed-form + ASSERT eyefishâ‰ fisheye for non-axis-symmetric input), VariationDescriptorTests.swift + ParamChannelParityTests.swift (bumpâ†’35), SpecialSauceParityTests.swift (testEyefish â‰Ą38dB), RealGenomeParityTests.swift (correct the "(alias of fisheye)" comment at :77).

**Acceptance Criteria:** CPU closed-form passes; eyefishâ‰ fisheye assertion passes (un-swapped); Metalâ†”CPU â‰Ą38dB; countâ†’35; make test-fast green; goldens unchanged.


tasks.subject: V3: port pie (3 params, 3 ordered RNG draws)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Port flam3 `pie` (var37, variations.c:795-809; params pie_slices=6/pie_rotation=0/pie_thickness=0.5; 3 isaac_01 draws in EXACT order). RNG-consuming â†’ goes in the `evaluate` switch, NOT the table. Unblocks 00000 (partially).

**3 draws in order:** (1) `sl=(Int)(isaac_01*slices+0.5)`; (2) `isaac_01*thickness` angular offset; (3) `weight*isaac_01` radial. Then `a = rotation + 2Ď€*(Double(sl)+d2*thickness)/slices; r = weight*d3; return (r*cos(a), r*sin(a))`.

**Files:** VariationDescriptor.swift (canonicalOrder + descriptor; countâ†’36), Variations.swift (evaluate switch + static `pie(_:weight:params:rng:)` mirroring julian; 3 rng.isaac01() in order), Kernels.metal (countâ†’36, v_pie(p,w,pr,rng) with 3 isaac_01(rng) in order + dispatch `if (w[35]!=0) acc += v_pie(pre,w[35],&x.varParams[35*8],rng)`), VariationsTests.swift (closed-form fixed-stream + draw-count-3 test per testJulianDrawsOneAndFinite), VariationDescriptorTests.swift + ParamChannelParityTests.swift (bumpâ†’36), SpecialSauceParityTests.swift (testPie â‰Ą38dB).

**Acceptance Criteria:** CPU draws exactly 3 isaac_01 in flam3 order (draw-count test); closed-form fixed-stream passes; Metalâ†”CPU â‰Ą38dB; in evaluate switch (not table); countâ†’36; make test-fast green.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["Sources/FlameKit/Variations.swift", "Sources/FlameRenderer/Metal/Kernels.metal", "Tests/FlameKitTests/VariationsTests.swift"], "verifyCommand": "swift test --filter VariationsTests --filter SpecialSauceParityTests", "acceptanceCriteria": ["pie 3 ordered draws", "pie CPU closed-form", "pie Metalâ†”CPU â‰Ą38dB", "in evaluate switch", "slot countâ†’36", "make test-fast green"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: V4: port radial_blur (1 param, 4 summed RNG draws)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Port flam3 `radial_blur` (var36, variations.c:775-793; param radial_blur_angle=0; 4 isaac_01 draws summed left-to-right). RNG-consuming â†’ evaluate switch. Unblocks 00000 (partially).

**4 draws summed:** `rndG = weight*(d1+d2+d3+d4-2.0)`. `spinvar=sin(angle*Ď€/2); zoomvar=cos(angle*Ď€/2); ra=sqrt(p.xÂ˛+p.yÂ˛); tmpa=atan2(p.y,p.x)+spinvar*rndG; rz=zoomvar*rndG-1; return (ra*cos(tmpa)+rz*p.x, ra*sin(tmpa)+rz*p.y)`.

**Files:** VariationDescriptor.swift (canonicalOrder + descriptor; countâ†’37), Variations.swift (evaluate switch + static `radialBlur(_:weight:params:rng:)`; 4 rng.isaac01() summed in order), Kernels.metal (countâ†’37, v_radial_blur(p,w,pr,rng) with 4 isaac_01(rng) summed + dispatch), VariationsTests.swift (closed-form + draw-count-4 test), VariationDescriptorTests.swift + ParamChannelParityTests.swift (bumpâ†’37), SpecialSauceParityTests.swift (testRadialBlur â‰Ą38dB).

**Acceptance Criteria:** CPU draws exactly 4 isaac_01 summed as weight*(d+d+d+d-2) (draw-count test); closed-form passes; Metalâ†”CPU â‰Ą38dB; countâ†’37; make test-fast green.

